commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `com.cmuxterm.app` and `cmux` to Trimmy's built-in terminal app detection identifiers.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main lacks `com.cmuxterm.app` and `cmux`, so `isTerminal(bundleIdentifier: "com.cmuxterm.app", appName: nil)` would fall through to false. I did not run the macOS app path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR appears correct but needs contributor or maintainer real behavior proof before merge; there is no narrow code repair for automation to perform.

target.title: Add cmux to terminal app detection
target.body:
## What

Add [cmux](https://github.com/steipete/cmux) to terminal app detection in `TerminalAppIdentifiers`.

## Why

cmux is a terminal emulator, but its bundle identifier `com.cmuxterm.app` was not in `exactBundleIdentifiers`, so `isTerminal(...)` returned `false` for it. Copies made inside cmux were therefore classified as **general-app** copies and trimmed with the *General* aggressiveness level (and skipped when General is set to *None*), instead of the *Terminal* level. In practice this meant terminal snippets copied in cmux were under-trimmed.

## Change

- Add the exact bundle id `com.cmuxterm.app` to `exactBundleIdentifiers`.
- Add a `"cmux"` entry to `nameHints` as a belt-and-suspenders fallback.

```swift
static func isTerminal(...) // "com.cmuxterm.app" -> true
```

## Validation

- Bundle id confirmed on macOS: `mdls`/`Info.plist` of `/Applications/cmux.app` â†’ `CFBundleIdentifier = com.cmuxterm.app`.
- Data-only addition (string literals into a `Set`/`Array`); covered by existing `ci.yml`.

đź¤– Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
